terms.definition: The core storage object. May represent an event, place, source-linked claim, archive reference, timeline entry, organization, dataset snapshot, cultural memory, or egress artifact.
Where a claim, dataset, record, or reference came from.
A statement made by a source. Claims are stored separately from normalized records.
Provenance packet showing when something was observed, from where, by what method, and with what parser or reviewer.
One of the 42 civic-memory categories used for classification and routing.
Observer 43. Reviews provenance, rights, place, time, uncertainty, sensitivity, and egress rules.
A produced output, such as a seed article, dashboard card, timeline, brief, civic packet, or research note.
A summary that avoids private data, unsafe amplification, rights violations, and unsupported claims.
A broad historical layer used when exact dates are unknown or when records span eras.
How precise and reliable the record date is.
Reuse and storage posture for the source or record.
Whether and how a record may appear in public outputs.

local_aliases.notes: Use as organization alias only. Do not treat all SPS records as final truth.
Use as organization alias for fire and rescue sources.
Use only when source context is clear.
May not be identical to casual use of downtown. Requires place normalization.

local_aliases.expands_to: Downtown Saskatoon or Central Business District depending on context
local_aliases.notes: Requires source and geography check before normalization.
Use carefully. May involve vulnerable-person data and should default to heightened review.
